title: Rosemary scalp oil
subtitle: A light scalp treatment oil with rosemary and cedarwood essential oils
excerpt: Rosemary essential oil has been studied for its effect on scalp circulation. Combined with cedarwood and fractionated coconut oil as a lightweight carrier, this blend applies to the scalp before washing and is rinsed out after 30 minutes. The light base oil absorbs readily and does not leave residue.
sourceNotes: Rosemary EO scalp use from Tisserand Institute research summary (tisserandinstitute.org, May 2026). EO dilution rates from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014). Fractionated coconut oil properties from supplier datasheet.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. 50 ml is approximately 4 to 6 applications.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in advance and store in a dropper or pump bottle. Shake before each use to redistribute the essential oils.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 18 months in a cool, dark place. Fractionated coconut oil has a very long shelf life; the limiting factor is the essential oils, which oxidise over time. Rosemary EO should be used within 18 months of opening.

recipeTools.notes: Or a small jug for blending

glossaryTerms.definition: Coconut oil processed to remain liquid at room temperature by removing the longer-chain fatty acids. Very lightweight, fast-absorbing, and long-lasting on the shelf. Used as a carrier for scalp and body oil blends.

content.text: Rosemary essential oil (Rosmarinus officinalis ct. camphor or ct. verbenone) is one of the most commonly studied essential oils for scalp health. A 2023 clinical trial found rosemary oil comparable to minoxidil 2% for hair density in subjects with androgenetic alopecia. The mechanism is not fully understood but may involve improved scalp microcirculation.
is the lightest available carrier: it does not weigh hair down or leave scalp residue after washing, which makes it the best base for a pre-wash scalp treatment.

Avoid this formula during the first trimester of pregnancy and if you have epilepsy: rosemary essential oil contains camphor and should be used with caution in these cases. Toxic to cats. Apply to the scalp only, not the lengths of the hair.

items.prepNote: The lightweight carrier; absorbs without residue
1% dilution; adds a woody note and supports the blend

content.text: Combine all three ingredients in a small jug. Stir briefly. Pour into a dropper bottle or a small pump bottle. To use, apply 5 to 10 drops directly to a dry or slightly damp scalp, section by section. Massage in with fingertips for 2 minutes. Leave for at least 30 minutes (or overnight under a shower cap) before washing out with shampoo. Use two to three times per week for best results.
18 months in a cool, dark place. Shake before each use. Discard if the oil develops a rancid or stale smell.
